titleStmt.title: SII 26.404: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 404. (A.R. No. 383 of 1909.) SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription records the sale of land, 700 kuḻi in extent, as tax-free dēvadāna by the mahāsabhai of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukaiy-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu for an amount of money (not specified) agreed to by the sabhai, to a certain Āḍavallāṉ Vāsudēvan alias Muḍikoṇḍaśōḻa Mūvēndavēḷāṉ, the madhyastha of Muññūṟu alias Paṇḍitagōshṭhi-saruppēdimaṅgalam in Ōymā-nāḍu. It is stated that he endowed it to provide for the services and offerings connected with the procession on every Sunday and the morning worship of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tribhuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikkiramaco
ḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu 4 Āvatu malāṭāṉa jaṉaṉātavaḷanāṭṭu
k kuṟukaiy kūṟṟattu brahmadeyañ ciṟṟiṅkūr mahā
sabhaiyom Ivvūr Āḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavade
varkku devatānamāka viṟṟukkuṭutta nilamāvatu
taṭi 4lukku kiḻpāṟkellai puttaṟaikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkel
lai Itdevadeva paḷḷicceykku vaṭakkum melpāṟke
llai meṭṭu velattaṅkālukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai Ā
ḻvār tevatānattukkum rājarājan vāykkālukku teṟkum Āka
nārper Ellaiyuḷ Akappaṭa nilattil paḻandavatānam kaṇa
patiyā kuṇṭil kuḻi Aimpatu nikki niṉṟa kuḻi Eḻuṉūṟum Uḷnila
moḻivinṟi Itdevar tirunāyaṟṟukiḻamai Eḻun=taruḷukaikkum Amu
tupaṭi Arici patakkum kaṟiyamutu Oṉṟum neyyamutu Uḻakkum tai
yiramutu Irunāḻiyum Uppamutu Uḻakkum Aṭaikkāyamutu pākku pattu
m veṟṟilaiyamutu Irupatum tiruviḷakkeṇṇai nāḻiyum tirunāya
ṟṟukkiḻamaiy toṟum ciṟukālai sandhi candrādityavaṟ celvatā
ka Oymānāṭṭu muññūṟāṉa paṇḍitagoṣṭhisaruppetimaṅkalat
tu maddhyasthana Āṭavallāṉ vāsudevanāṉa muṭikoṇṭa
coḻa muven=taveḷāṉ Iṉṉilattukku Emmilicain=ta vilaippo
poruḷ Aṟakkoṇṭu Immuṭikoṇṭacoḻa muven=taveḷāṉ dha
rmmamāka kuṭuttom mahāsabhaiyom Itu panmāheśvara[rakṣai §]
Digital edition of SII 26.404 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
